name: Baked Salmon in Foil
recipeInstructions:
1. Lay a piece of silver foil, large enough to wrap the salmon in a very loose parcel, on a baking tray.
2. If serving hot, grease the foil with butter; for cold servings, use oil instead.
3. Thinly slice the lemon and place about half on the foil.
4. Place the salmon on top, adding a few slices of lemon inside, then lay the remaining lemon along the top of the salmon.
5. Season generously and wrap loosely, ensuring the edges are securely folded to prevent steam from escaping.
6. Bake in a medium oven (Gas Mark 4, 350°F/180°C) for 10 minutes per pound.
7. If serving hot, remove after 10 minutes and let stand for an additional 10 minutes.
8. If serving cold, leave the salmon wrapped for two to three hours.
9. To serve, carefully unwrap the fish and gently pull off the skin.
